sources.title: Earth's Orbit - NASA
sources.content: Earth orbits the Sun at an average distance of 93 million miles, completing one orbit every 365.25 days.

sources.title: Solar System - Britannica
sources.content: Earth is the third planet from the Sun and completes one revolution around it annually.

description: Well-established scientific fact with supporting sources should score high

sources.title: Flat Earth Debunked - Scientific American
sources.content: The flat Earth claim has been thoroughly debunked. Evidence includes satellite imagery and circumnavigation proving Earth is spherical.

sources.title: Earth's Shape - NASA
sources.content: NASA satellites have photographed Earth from space countless times, clearly showing its spherical shape.

description: Debunked claim with contradicting sources should score low

claims: Coffee is good for your health.

sources.title: Coffee Health Benefits - Harvard Health
sources.content: Studies suggest moderate coffee consumption may reduce risk of certain diseases including type 2 diabetes and Parkinson's.

sources.title: Coffee Risks - Mayo Clinic
sources.content: Excessive coffee consumption can lead to anxiety, insomnia, and increased heart rate. Some people should limit intake.

description: Claim with mixed supporting and contradicting evidence

sources.title: Roswell Incident - History.com
sources.content: The Roswell incident involved a crashed weather balloon, according to the U.S. military. Conspiracy theories persist but no evidence of aliens has been confirmed.

description: Claim that cannot be definitively verified with available sources

claims: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level.
Water is wet because of hydrogen bonds.

sources.title: Boiling Point - Chemistry LibreTexts
sources.content: At standard atmospheric pressure (sea level), pure water boils at 100°C (212°F).

sources.title: Properties of Water - USGS
sources.content: Water molecules form hydrogen bonds, giving water its unique properties like high surface tension.

description: Multiple claims with varying levels of support
